Salary Reduction Agreement shall not exceed nine
thousand five hundred dollars (9,500.00) for the taxable

shall be adjusted for the cost-of-living in accordance with

respect to whom the excess contribution is made and shall

(2) notwithstanding any other provision of this Plan, if
the participant allocates any portion of contributions in
excess of the limitation imposed in the immediately
foregoing Subsection (d)(i) for a taxable year to the
Participant’s Account no later than the first March 1
following the close of the taxable year, as described in
Code Section 402(g)(2)(A), then the Trustees may
distribute such excess deferrals, plus any income or minus
any losses allocable to them, to the Participant no later
than the first April 15 following the close of the taxable
year. The Participant is solely responsible to timely make
the foregoing allocation.

(3) Neither the Trustees nor any person or organization
engaged by them to provide administrative services in
connection with this Plan have any duty to determine
whether contributions to any Participant’s Account for
any calendar year comply with the limitation imposed in
the foregoing Subsection (d)(i) or to assure that they do
comply, or to assure that any excess deferrals are timely
allocated to the Participant’s Account.

Sections 6.01-6.04 are amended by replacing these Sections and the heading
preceding them with the following:

PERMITTED DISTRIBUTIONS AND TRANSFERS

6.01 Request for Distributions. To receive distributions, a
participant or beneficiary must file a written election on a form provided
by the Trustees which designates the manner and time for distributions
as permitted under Article VII. The Trustees have the authority to
approve or disapprove a distribution election before the distribution is
made.

<a id="ga28-p489"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=491 printed_page=489 -->

6.02 Events Permitting Distributions of Employee
Contributions. Except as otherwise provided by this Plan, a Participant
may begin receiving distributions of funds from the Participant’s
Account that are attributable to contributions made pursuant to a Salary
Reduction Agreement (including earnings thereon) only if:

• the Participant has attained age 59 lA;
• the Participant has separated from Service (as provided in Section
12.16);
• the participant has become disabled within the meaning of code
Section 72(m)(7); or
• the Participant suffers financial hardship as provided in Section 6.08
below, except that hardship does not entitle a Participant to receive
earnings on such contributions.

6.03 Permitted Distributions of Employer Contributions.
after any plan valuation a participant may elect to receive a distribution
of all or any portion of his Account, excluding the portion of his account
consisting of salary reduction contributions and earnings thereon.

6.04 Distributions Subject to Penalty Excise Tax. Any
distribution, including a distribution of employee contributions described
in Section 6.02 ora distribution of employer contributions described in
Section 6.03, is subject to an excise tax of 10% of the amount included in
gross income unless it qualifies as exempt from such tax under Code
Section 72(t). Such exempt distributions include distributions:

• made on or after the date on which the Participant attains age 59-1/2;
• made to the Beneficiary (or to the estate of the Participant) on or
after the death of the Participant;
• attributable to the employee's being disabled within the meaning of
Code Section 72(m)(7);
• that are part of a series of substantially equal periodic payments (not
less frequently than annually) made for the life (or life expectancy)
of the participant or the joint lives (or joint life expectancies) of the
Participant and his designated beneficiary, beginning after the
Participant separates from service;
• made to the Participant after separation from service after attainment
of age 55; or
• made to alternate payees pursuant to qualified domestic relations
orders.

Neither the Trustees nor any person or organization engaged by them to provide
administrative services in connection with the Plan have any duty to determine whether
any distribution is subject to or exempt from the excise tax described above. The

<a id="ga28-p490"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=492 printed_page=490 -->

Participant bears the responsibility to make that determination and to pay any excise tax
arising from any distribution.

A new Section 6.05 is inserted to read as follows and the remaining sections are
renumbered accordingly:
6.05 distributions to beneficiaries after death of Participant. When
a Participant dies, the Participant's Beneficiary is entitled to a benefit
equal to the Participant's Account balance. The Beneficiary must
complete an election form provided by the Trustees. Any balance
remaining in the Participant's account on the death of the beneficiary
will be paid to the Beneficiary's estate unless a survivor is designated by
the Participant or by the Beneficiary, provided that any designation by
the Participant cannot be modified by the Beneficiary. payment of death
benefits to a Participant's Beneficiary will commence as soon as
administratively feasible following the Beneficiary's filing of a claim for
benefits with the Trustees.

Old Section 6.05 is amended to read as follows:
6.06 Cash-Out of Small Accounts. Notwithstanding any other
provision of this Plan, the Trustees may, in their sole discretion, require
payment ina lumps um of the value of the account of any participant
who separates from Service with the Employer if the balance in his
Account, as of the date of his separation from Service, is less than
$2,500.00. Furthermore, a Participant who has separated from Service
with the employer may elect to receive a lumps um distribution of his
Account following any plan valuation, provided that request for such
withdrawal has been received by the Trustees no later than the date
established by the trustees for the receipt of such distribution requests.
any such payments will extinguish all rights of the cashed-out
Participant to benefits under this Plan.

Old Section 6.08 is amended by deleting Paragraph (a) (moved to another
section), replacing in old paragraph (b)(1) “hardship distribution of up to 80%”
with ‘Tinancial Hardship Distribution”, and making old Paragraph (c) a new
Section 6.09

A new Section 6.13 is inserted to read as follows:
Qualified Domestic Relations Order. Notwithstanding any other
provision of this Plan, subject to the disapproval of the Trustees,
distributions may be made pursuant to qualified domestic relations
orders. for purposes of this Plan, a “qualified domestic relations order”
is a “domestic relations order” within the meaning of code section
414(pXl)(B) that meets the requirement of code section 414(p)(A)(i).

<a id="ga28-p491"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=493 printed_page=491 -->

Sections 7.01-7.06 are amended by replacing these Sections and the heading
preceding them with the following:

MANNER OF AND TIME FOR DISTRIBUTIONS

7.01 Distributions to Participant. A Participant may elect to
receive distributions in one ora combination of the following ways:

• in a lump-sum payment (subject to Section 7.05 below);
• in installment payments paid monthly, or at some other interval no less
frequent than annually approved by the Trustees, over a period certain
not to exceed the life expectancy of the participant or the joint and last
survivor life expectancy of the participant and the designated
Beneficiary; or
• if permitted by the Trustees, as a life annuity or an annuity for the life of
the participant with a survivor annuity for the life of the designated
Beneficiary (which may be purchased from a licensed insurance
company and either distributed to the Participant or retained in the Trust
fund and used as the source of funding payments from the trust to the
Participant and the Participant’s Beneficiaries.

the amounts to be distributed each year under any of the foregoing
elections must comply with Section 7.02 below.

7.02 Required Minimum Distributions.
(a) Required Beginning Date. notwithstanding any provision of
this Plan to the contrary, distributions of the Participant’s
Account must commence no later than April 1 of the calendar
year following the later of (i) the calendar year in which the
Participant attains age 70 1/2, and (ii) the calendar year in which
the Participant retires from Service. The April 1 so determined is
the “Required Beginning Date” for purposes of this article VII.

(b) Beneficiary. Required minimum distributions are determined
by using the life expectancy of the beneficiary of the Participant,
if any, who qualifies for such purpose under Code Section
401(a)(9) and the Regulations thereunder. The Beneficiary so
determined is the “Designated Beneficiary” for purposes of this
Article VII.

(c) Minimum Amounts to be Distributed. The minimum amount
required to be distributed to the Participant for each taxable year,
beginning no later than the Required Beginning Date, is the
amount determined by dividing the total amount credited to the
Participant’s account as of the beginning of the taxable year

<a id="ga28-p492"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=494 printed_page=492 -->

(reduced for the taxable year containing the Required Beginning
Date by any minimum distribution for the previous year that is
made during the taxable year containing the Required Beginning
Date but no later than that date) by the life expectancy of the
participant or the joint and last survivor life expectancy of the
Participant and the Designated Beneficiary. Life expectancy and
joint and last survivor life expectancy are computed by using the
return multiples in Regulations Section 1.72-9. The life
expectancy of the participant and if applicable the Participant’s
spouse Designated Beneficiary may be recalculated, not more
frequently than annually; the life expectancy of the Participant’s
non-spouse Designated Beneficiary may not be recalculated. A
Participant must elect whether to recalculate life expectancies
prior to receiving his first required distribution, and if he makes
no election by then, the Participant’s and any spouse Designated
Beneficiary’s life expectancies will be recalculated. When life
expectancies are being recalculated, a Participant’s (or the spouse
Designated Beneficiary’s) life expectancy is reduced to zero in
the year following the year of death.

(d) Payment Dates. The first year for which a distribution must
be made is the later of (i) the calendar year in which the
Participant attains age 70 1/2, and (ii) the calendar year in which
the Participant retires from Service. The minimum distribution
for that base year must be distributed completely no later than the
required beginning date under the preceding Subsection (a). The
minimum distribution for each subsequent year must be
distributed by December 31 of that year.

7.03 Distributions after the Death of a Participant.

(a) Beneficiary’s Election. If the Participant dies before his or
her Account has been completely distributed, the Beneficiary
may elect to receive all assets remaining in the Account in one or
a combination of the following ways, subject to the terms of any
annuity elected by the participant as the manner of distributions
to be made to the Participant before his or her death:
• in a lump-sum payment (subject to Section 7.05 below),
or
• in installment payments paid monthly, or at some other
interval no less frequent than annually approved by the
Trustees, over a period certain not to exceed the life
expectancy of the participant or the joint and last survivor
life expectancy of the participant and the designated
Beneficiary.

<a id="ga28-p493"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=495 printed_page=493 -->

the amounts to be distributed each year under any of the
foregoing elections must comply with the minimum distribution
requirements of code section 401(a)(9) and the following
subsections of this section 7.03.

(b) Distributions to a Beneficiary When Participant Dies Before
Required Beginning Date If the Participant dies before the
Required Beginning Date, distributions must be made in one of
two ways elected by the Beneficiary:
• no later than 5 years after the death of the Participant, or
• over the life expectancy of the designated Beneficiary.
the beneficiary must irrevocably elect one of the foregoing no
later than December 31 of the year in which distributions must
commence. If the Beneficiary does not make the foregoing
election, distributions must be made over the life expectancy of
the Designated Beneficiary. If the Beneficiary elects to receive
distributions over the life expectancy of the designated
Beneficiary, distributions must commence on or before
December 31 of the calendar year immediately following the
calendar year in which the Participant died

(c) Distributions to a Spouse Beneficiary When Participant Dies
Before Required Beginning Date. Distributions to a Participant’s
Beneficiary when the Designated Beneficiary is the surviving
spouse must be made in the same manner as distributions when
the Designated Beneficiary is not the spouse as described above,
except for the following special rules.
(1) Later Commencement: Distributions must be made in
one of two way selected by the spouse:
• no later than 5 years after the death of the Participant;
or
• over the life expectancy of the spouse.
the spouse must irrevocably elect one of the foregoing no
later than the earlier of (i) December 31 of the year in
which distributions must commence and (ii) December 31
of the calendar year which contains the fifth anniversary
of the date of the Participant’s death. If the spouse does
not make the foregoing election, distributions must be
made over the spouse’s life expectancy. If the spouse
elects to receive distributions over the spouse’s life
expectancy, distributions must commence on or before the
later of (i) December 31 of the calendar year immediately
following the calendar year in which the Participant died
and (ii) December 31 of the calendar year in which the
Participant would have attained age 70-1/2.

<a id="ga28-p494"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=496 printed_page=494 -->

(2) Treatment as Participant: If the spouse dies after the
Participant but before the spouse is required to begin
receiving distributions, the spouse’s Designated
Beneficiary may receive distributions as though the
spouse had been the Participant For these purposes, the
Designated Beneficiary is treated as a non-spouse
Designated Beneficiary even if he or she is the spouse’s
new spouse.

(d) Distributions to a Beneficiary When Participant Dies On or
After Required Beginning Date. If the Participant died on or after
the Required Beginning Date determined under the preceding
Section 7.02(a), distributions must continue, and the minimum
amount to be distributed for any taxable year is the amount
determined under the preceding Section 7.02(b) (the amount
determined by dividing the total amount credited to the
Participant’s account as of the beginning of the taxable year by
the joint and last survivor life expectancy of the participant and
the Designated Beneficiary).

7.04 Necessary Compliance with Code Section 401(a)(9). All
distributions must comply with the statutory rules of code section
401(a)(9), including the incidental death benefit requirement in Code
Section 401(a)(9)(G). All distributions will be made in accordance with
the Regulations under Code Section 401(a)(9), including Regulations
Section 1.401(a)(9)-2. the provisions of this plan reflecting code
Section 401(a)(9) override any distribution options in the Plan that are
inconsistent with that section.

7.05 Lump Sum Payments. The Trustees have the authority to
approve or disapprove a Participant’s or Beneficiary’s election to receive
distributions in a lump sum. a participant may request payment of one
or more of his accounts as a lumps um and payment of his remaining
accounts ina manner permitted under the other provisions of this article
VII, and may request an initial payment of apart of any account as a
cash distribution followed by payment of the remainder of such account
ina manner permitted under the other provisions of this article VII. The
trustees may deny or delay authorization of payment of lumps um sin
their discretion, and the exercise of such discretion to prevent the trust
fund from incurring losses upon liquidation of trust fund assets is a
proper exercise of this discretion.

7.06 Distribution of Earnings in Final Distribution Year.
Any Trust Fund earnings allocable to the Participant's Accounts for the
calendar year in which a lump sum distribution or the last installment

<a id="ga28-p495"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=497 printed_page=495 -->

distribution is made may be paid to the Participant (or the Participant’s
Beneficiary as applicable) in a separate distribution after the amount of
such earnings has been determined.

Section 7.07 is amended to read as follows:
Additional Methods of Benefit Distribution. The Trustees have
discretion to permit payment of retirement benefits in any manner or
form not specifically described in this Article VII, provided that all
distributions must comply with the rules in Sections 7.02 and 7.03 and
any other applicable requirements of the code or Regulations.

Section 7.08 is amended to read as follows:
Changes in Distributions. A Participant or Beneficiary who is
receiving distributions may, subject to the following restrictions, elect to
change the amount, manner or form of distributions. The following
restrictions apply:
(a) The change must be an amount, manner, or
form permitted under the provisions of the Plan.
(b) Any change in amount must continue to
comply with the rules in Sections 7.02 and 7.03 and any other
applicable requirements of the code or Regulations.
(c) All changes must be made in writing and
are subject to the approval of the plan Administrator.
(d) No more than one election to change may
be made in any Plan Year.
(e) The Plan Administrator shall have
discretion to adopt appropriate rules for the administration
requests made under this Section 7.07.

Section 7.09 is amended by modifying the first sentence to read as follows:
“Notwithstanding any other provision of this Plan, the amount distributed to a
Participant for any calendar year in which the Participant’s Designated
Beneficiary is not the spouse must satisfy the Minimum Distribution Incidental
Benefit Requirement described in this Section.”

Old Section 7.09(a) is deleted

Old Section 7.09(b)(1) is amended by modifying the first and second paragraphs
to read:
Distributions Not Paid from an Annuity Contract. For any calendar year
beginning after December 31, 1988, if benefits are not paid byway of an
insured annuity contract, the Minimum Distribution Requirement will be
satisfied if:
(1) distribution of a Participant’s benefit commences not later than April
1 of the calendar year following the later of the calendar year in which

<a id="ga28-p496"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=498 printed_page=496 -->

the Participant attains age 70 1/2, and the calendar year in which the Participant retires from Service (the later of these years being identified in this Section 7.09 as the “Base Year”); and.

Old Section 7.09(b) is further amended by replacing “calendar year in which the Participant attains age 70 ½” every place it appears after the first instance with “Base Year”.

Old Section 7.09(b)(2) is amended to read as follows:

(b) **Distributions Paid from an Annuity Contract.** If benefits are paid by way of an insured annuity contract, the Minimum Distribution Requirement will be satisfied if:

(1) Distribution of a Participant’s benefit commences not later than April 1 of the calendar year following the Base Year; and
(2) The amount distributed with respect to the Base Year and each calendar year thereafter is not less than the minimum amount required to be distributed under Section 401(a)(9) of the Code and the Regulations thereunder.

Section 8.01 is amended by replacing “Filing of a Claim” with “Filing of Request”, and replacing “claim for Plan benefits” with “request for distributions”.

Section 8.02 is amended by replacing “Denial of Claim” with “Denial of Request”, replacing “claim” the next three places it appears with “request”, and replacing “Plan’s claim procedure” with “Plan’s distribution request procedure”.

Section 8.04 is amended by modifying the last sentence to read: “All decisions on and denials of requests for distributions by the Trustees shall be afforded the maximum deference permitted by law.”

A new Section 12.16 is inserted to read as follows:

**Separation from Service.** For purposes of this Plan, a Participant who is an ordained Teaching Elder in the Presbyterian Church in America shall be considered to have separated or retired from Service, or from all remunerative Service, with the Employer, or to have terminated Service with the Employer, only when such Teaching Elder is either:

- No longer a member of any presbytery of the Presbyterian Church in America, or
- Honorably retired from his Presbytery.

Any other Participant shall be considered to have so separated, retired, or terminated whenever any employment of the Participant with an Employer terminates.

<a id="ga28-p497"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=499 printed_page=497 -->

The Entire Plan is amended by modifying its spacing, numbering, headings,
and style and correcting typographical and other errors, as reflected in the
revised Plan document presented to the Trustees

eleventh amendment
TO TH E
Presbyterian Church in America
MONEY PU RCHASE PENSION PLAN

this amendment to the Presbyterian Church in America money purchase
Pension Plan (hereinafter referred to as the "Plan"), made this 5th day of November,
1999 by the board of trustees of the Insurance, annuity and relief funds of the
Presbyterian Church in America (hereinafter referred to as the "Trustees"), to be
effective as noted below,
WITNESSETH:
where a S, the Presbyterian Church in America sponsors and maintains the
Plan as a qualified plan under Section 401(a) of the internal revenue code of 1986, as
amended (“Code”), and a church plan under Section 414(e) of such Code, and
where a S, Section 11.01 of the plan reserves to the trustees the right to alter
or amend the plan subject to the approval of the General Assembly of the Presbyterian Church in America; and
where a S, it is the desire of the trustees to amend the plan to clarify the
Trustees’ prior and continued understanding that separation or retirement from service
in the case of a plan participant who is a minister ordained within the Presbyterian Church in America occurs only when such minister is no longer a member of any
presbytery of the Presbyterian Church in America, or is honorably retired from his
presbytery.
NOW , therefore , IT IS RESOLVED that the Plan be amended in the
form attached hereto as exhibit a effective as of the date or dates set forth in such
Exhibit, and it is
further resolved , that the director of the Insurance, Annuity and
relief funds of the Presbyterian Church in America is hereby authorized to take any
and all actions necessary and desirable to carry out this and the foregoing resolution.
in witness where o F, the Trustees have caused this Eleventh
Amendment to be executed on the day and year first above written.
TRUSTEES:
BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE
INSURANCE, ANNUITY AND RELIEF
FUNDS OF THE PRESBYTERIAN CHURCH IN AMERICA

EXHIBIT A

The Plan is hereby amended as follows, to be effective as of november 5, 1999:
Section 6 01 shall be amended by adding a third paragraph to the end to read as
follows:
for purposes of this article VI, a Participant who is an ordained
Teaching Elder in the Presbyterian Church in America shall be
considered to have separated or retired from Service, or from all
remunerative Service, with the Employer, or to have terminated Service
with the Employer, only when such Teaching Elder is either:

- **c)** no longer a member of an ypres byte ry of the Presbyterian Church in America, or
- **d)** Honorably retired from his Presbytery.

TWELFTH AMENDMENT
TO THE
PRESBYTERIAN CHURCH EN AMERICA
MONEY PURCHASE PENSION PLAN

THIS AMENDMENT to the Presbyterian Church in America Money Purchase
Pension Plan (hereinafter referred to as the “Plan”), made this 3rd day of March, 2000,
by the board of trustees of the Insurance, annuity and relief funds of the Presbyterian Church in America (hereinafter referred to as the “Trustees”), to be effective as noted
below,

WITNESSETH:

WHEREAS, the Presbyterian Church in America sponsors and maintains the
Plan as a qualified plan under Section 401(a) of the internal revenue code of 1986, as
amended (“Code”), and a church plan under Section 414(e) of such Code, and

WHEREAS, Section 11.01 of the plan reserves to the trustees the right to alter
or amend the plan subject to the approval of the General Assembly of the Presbyterian Church in America; and

<a id="ga28-p499"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=501 printed_page=499 -->

WHEREAS, it is the desire of the Trustees to amend the Plan to expand and simplify the distribution options available under the Plan, to cause the text of the Plan to follow more closely the text of the Internal Revenue Code and regulations thereunder, and to make other changes to the Plan;

NOW THEREFORE, IT IS RESOLVED that the Plan be amended as provided in Exhibit A, conditional upon issuance by the Internal Revenue Service of a determination letter that the Plan as so amended meets the requirements of a qualified defined contribution pension plan under Code Section 401(a) and the requirements of a church plan under Code Section 414(e), and with the modifications the Internal Revenue Service may require before it will issue the determination letter, effective as of the date of the determination letter; and it is

FURTHER RESOLVED, that the Director of the Insurance, Annuity and Relief Funds of the Presbyterian Church in America is hereby authorized to take any and all actions necessary and desirable to carry out this and the foregoing resolution, including making any modifications to the Plan required by the Internal Revenue Service.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Trustees have caused this Twelfth Amendment to be executed on the day and year first above written.

TRUSTEES:
BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE
INSURANCE, ANNUITY AND
RELIEF FUNDS OF THE
PRESBYTERIAN CHURCH IN AMERICA

By: _________________________
By: _________________________
By: _________________________
By: _________________________
By: _________________________
By: _________________________
By: _________________________
By: _________________________
By: _________________________
By: _________________________
By: _________________________

# EXHIBIT A

The Plan is hereby amended as follows:

<a id="ga28-p500"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=502 printed_page=500 -->

Section 1.03 is amended by modifying the last sentence to read as follows:
“Section 12.07 shall apply to determine the Beneficiary if neither the
Beneficiary nor the Contingent Beneficiary designated by the Participant
survives the Participant or if no Beneficiary or Contingent Beneficiary has been
effectively named .”

Section 1.07 is amended by replacing “designated Beneficiary” with
“Beneficiary”.

Section 1.09 is amended to read as follows:
The term "Employer" shall mean a Church, or any other employer which
employs in connection with his ministry a duly ordained minister of the
Presbyterian Church in America engaged in the exercise of his ministry
with the approval of the appropriate presbytery, which has adopted this
plan by execution of an adoption agreement which has been consented
to by the Trustees.

Sections 6.01-6.04 are amended by replacing these Sections and the heading
preceding them with the following:
PERMITTED DISTRIBUTIONS AND TRANSFERS

6.01 Request for Distributions. To receive distributions, a
participant or beneficiary must file a written election on a form provided
by the Trustees which designates the manner and time for distributions
as permitted under Article VII. The Trustees have the authority to
approve or disapprove a distribution election before the distribution is
made.

6.02 Events Permitting Distributions of Employee
Contributions. Except as otherwise provided by this Plan, a Participant
may begin receiving distributions of funds from the Participant’s
Account, other than those attributable to

Nondeductible Employee Contributions made pursuant to Section 4.03,
only if:
• the Participant has attained age 65;
• the Participant has separated from Service (as provided in
Section 12.16), or
• the participant has become disabled within the meaning of
Code Section 72(m)(7)

6.03 Distributions Subject to Penalty Excise Tax. Any
distribution is subject to an excise tax of 10% of the amount included in
gross income unless it qualifies as exempt from such tax under Code
Section 72(t). Such exempt distributions include distributions:

<a id="ga28-p501"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=503 printed_page=501 -->

• made on or after the date on which the Participant attains
age 59-1/2;
• made to the Beneficiary (or to the estate of the
Participant) on or after the death of the Participant,
• attributable to the employee's being disabled within the
meaning of code section 72(m)(7);
• that are part of a series of substantially equal periodic
payments (not less frequently than annually) made for the
life (or life expectancy) of the participant or the joint lives
(or joint life expectancies) of the Participant and his
designated beneficiary, beginning after the Participant
separates from service,
• made to the Participant after separation from service after
attainment of age 55, or
• made to alternate payees pursuant to qualified domestic
relations orders.
Neither the Trustees nor any person or organization engaged by them to
provide administrative services in connection with the Plan have any
duty to determine whether any distribution is subject to or exempt from
the excise tax described above. The Participant bears the responsibility
to make that determination and to pay any excise tax arising from any
distribution

6.04 Distributions to Beneficiaries after Death of Participant.
When a Participant dies, the Participant's beneficiary is entitled to a
benefit equal to the Participant's Account balance. The Beneficiary must
complete an election form provided by the Trustees. Any balance
remaining in the Participant's account on the death of the beneficiary
will be paid to the Beneficiary's estate unless a survivor is designated by
the Participant or by the Beneficiary, provided that any designation by
the participant cannot be modified by the beneficiary payment of death
benefits to a Participant's Beneficiary will commence as soon as
administratively feasible following the Beneficiary's filing of a claim for
benefits with the Trustees

Section 6.05 is amended by replacing “Church” with “Employer”

A new Section 6.11 is inserted to read as follows:
Qualified Domestic Relations Order. Notwithstanding any other
provision of this Plan, subject to the approval or disapproval of the
Trustees, distributions may be made pursuant to qualified domestic
relations orders. for purposes of this Plan, a “qualified domestic
relations order” is a “domestic relations order” within the meaning of
Code Section 414(p)(l)(B) that meets the requirement of code section
414(p)(lXA)(i).

<a id="ga28-p502"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=504 printed_page=502 -->

Sections 7.01-7.06 are amended by replacing these Sections and the heading
preceding them with the following:
MANNER OF AND TIME FOR DISTRIBUTIONS

7.01 Distributions to Participant. A Participant may elect to
receive distributions in one ora combination of the following ways:
• in a lump-sum payment (subject to Section 7.05 below),
• in installment payments paid monthly, or at some other
interval no less frequent than annually approved by the
Trustees, over a period certain not to exceed the life
expectancy of the participant or the joint and last survivor life
expectancy of the participant and the designated Beneficiary;
or
• if permitted by the Trustees, as a life annuity or an annuity for
the life of the participant with a survivor annuity for the life
of the designated beneficiary (which may be purchased from
a licensed insurance company and either distributed to the
Participant or retained in the Trust Fund and used as the
source of funding payments from the trust to the participant
and the Participant’s Beneficiaries
the amounts to be distributed each year under any of the foregoing
elections must comply with Section 7.02 below

7.02 Required Minimum Distributions.
(a) Required Beginning Date. Notwithstanding any provision of
this Plan to the contrary, distributions of the Participant’s
Account must commence no later than April 1 of the calendar
year following the later of (i) the calendar year in which the
Participant attains age 70 1/2, and (ii) the calendar year in which
the Participant retires from Service. The April 1 so determined is
the “Required Beginning Date” for purposes of this article vii

(b) Beneficiary. Required minimum distributions are determined
by using the life expectancy of the beneficiary of the Participant,
if any, who qualifies for such purpose under Code Section
401(a)(9) and the Regulations thereunder. The Beneficiary so
determined is the “Designated Beneficiary” for purposes of this
Article VII

(c) Minimum Amounts to be Distributed. The minimum amount
required to be distributed to the Participant for each taxable year,
beginning no later than the Required Beginning Date, is the
amount determined by dividing the total amount credited to the
Participant’s account as of the beginning of the taxable year

<a id="ga28-p503"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=505 printed_page=503 -->

(reduced for the taxable year containing the Required Beginning
Date by any minimum distribution for the previous year that is
made during the taxable year containing the Required Beginning
Date but no later than that date) by the life expectancy of the
participant or the joint and last survivor life expectancy of the
Participant and the Designated Beneficiary. Life expectancy and
joint and last survivor life expectancy are computed by using the
return multiples in Regulations Section 1.72-9. The life
expectancy of the participant and if applicable the Participant’s
spouse Designated Beneficiary may be recalculated, not more
frequently than annually; the life expectancy of the Participant’s
non-spouse Designated Beneficiary may not be recalculated A
Participant must elect whether to recalculate life expectancies
prior to receiving his first required distribution; and if he makes
no election by then, the Participant’s and any spouse Designated
Beneficiary’s life expectancies will be recalculated. When life
expectancies are being recalculated, a Participant’s (or the spouse
Designated Beneficiary’s) life expectancy is reduced to zero in
the year following the year of death.

(d) Payment Dates. The first year for which a distribution must
be made is the later of (i) the calendar year in which the
Participant attains age 70 1/2, and (ii) the calendar year in which
the Participant retires from Service. The minimum distribution
for that base year must be distributed completely no later than the
required beginning date under the preceding Subsection (a). The
minimum distribution for each subsequent year must be
distributed by December 31 of that year.

7.03 Distributions after the Death of a Participant.
(a) Beneficiary’s Election. If the Participant dies before his or
her Account has been completely distributed, the Beneficiary
may elect to receive all assets remaining in the Account in one or
a combination of the following ways, subject to the terms of any
annuity elected by the participant as the manner of distributions
to be made to the Participant before his or her death.
• in a lump-sum payment (subject to Section 7.05 below);
or
• in installment payments paid monthly, or at some other
interval no less frequent than annually approved by the
Trustees, over a period certain not to exceed the life
expectancy of the participant or the joint and last survivor
life expectancy of the participant and the designated
Beneficiary.

<a id="ga28-p504"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=506 printed_page=504 -->

the amounts to be distributed each year under any of the
foregoing elections must comply with the minimum distribution
requirements of code section 401(a)(9) and the following
subsections of this section 7.03.

(b) Distributions to a Beneficiary When Participant Dies Before
Required Beginning Date. If the Participant dies before the
Required Beginning Date, distributions must be made in one of
two ways elected by the Beneficiary:
• no later than 5 years after the death of the Participant, or
• over the life expectancy of the designated Beneficiary.
the beneficiary must irrevocably elect one of the foregoing no
later than December 31 of the year in which distributions must
commence. If the Beneficiary does not make the foregoing
election, distributions must be made over the life expectancy of
the Designated Beneficiary. If the Beneficiary elects to receive
distributions over the life expectancy of the designated
Beneficiary, distributions must commence on or before
December 31 of the calendar year immediately following the
calendar year in which the Participant died

(c) Distributions to a Spouse Beneficiary When Participant Dies
Before Required Beginning Date. Distributions to a Participant’s
Beneficiary when the Designated Beneficiary is the surviving
spouse must be made in the same manner as distributions when
the Designated Beneficiary is not the spouse as described above,
except for the following special rules.
(1) Later Commencement: Distributions must be made in
one of two way selected by the spouse:
• no later than 5 years after the death of the Participant,
or
• over the life expectancy of the spouse.
the spouse must irrevocably elect one of the foregoing no
later than the earlier of (i) December 31 of the year in
which distributions must commence and (ii) December 31
of the calendar year which contains the fifth anniversary
of the date of the Participant’s death. If the spouse does
not make the foregoing election, distributions must be
made over the spouse’s life expectancy. If the spouse
elects to receive distributions over the spouse’s life
expectancy, distributions must commence on or before the
later of (i) December 31 of the calendar year immediately
following the calendar year in which the Participant died
and (ii) December 31 of the calendar year in which the
Participant would have attained age 70-1/2.

<a id="ga28-p505"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=507 printed_page=505 -->

(2) Treatment as Participant: If the spouse dies after the
Participant but before the spouse is required to begin
receiving distributions, the spouse’s Designated
Beneficiary may receive distributions as though the
spouse had been the Participant. For these purposes, the
Designated Beneficiary is treated as a non-spouse
Designated Beneficiary even if he or she is the spouse’s
new spouse.

(d) Distributions to a Beneficiary When Participant Dies On or
After Required Beginning Date. If the Participant died on or after
the Required Beginning Date determined under the preceding
Section 7.02(a), distributions must continue, and the minimum
amount to be distributed for any taxable year is the amount
determined under the preceding Section 7.02(b) (the amount
determined by dividing the total amount credited to the
Participant’s account as of the beginning of the taxable year by
the joint and last survivor life expectancy of the participant and
the Designated Beneficiary).

7.04 Necessary Compliance with Code Section 401(a)(9). All
distributions must comply with the statutory rules of code section
401(a)(9), including the incidental death benefit requirement in Code
Section 401(a)(9)(G). All distributions will be made in accordance with
the Regulations under Code Section 401(a)(9), including Regulations
Section 1.401(a)(9)-2. the provisions of this plan reflecting code
Section 401(aX9) override any distribution options in the Plan that are
inconsistent with that section.

7.05 Lump Sum Payments. The Trustees have the authority to
approve or disapprove a Participant’s or Beneficiary’s election to receive
distributions in a lump sum. a participant may request payment of one
or more of his accounts as a lumps um and payment of his remaining
accounts ina manner permitted under the other provisions of this article
VII, and may request an initial payment of apart of any account as a
cash distribution followed by payment of the remainder of such account
ina manner permitted under the other provisions of this article VII. The
trustees may deny or delay authorization of payment of lumps um sin
their discretion, and the exercise of such discretion to prevent the trust
fund from incurring losses upon liquidation of trust fund assets is a
proper exercise of this discretion.

7.06 Distribution of Earnings in Final Distribution Year.
Any Trust Fund earnings allocable to the Participant's Accounts for the
calendar year in which a lump sum distribution or the last installment

<a id="ga28-p506"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=508 printed_page=506 -->

distribution is made may be paid to the Participant (or the Participant’s
Beneficiary as applicable) in a separate distribution after the amount of
such earnings has been determined.

Section 7.07 is amended to read as follows:
Additional Methods of Benefit Distribution The Trustees have
discretion to permit payment of retirement benefits in any manner or
form not specifically described in this Article VII, provided that all
distributions must comply with the rules in Sections 7.02 and 7.03 and
any other applicable requirements of the code or Regulations.

Section 7.08 is amended to read as follows:
Changes in Distributions. A Participant or Beneficiary who is
receiving distributions may, subject to the following restrictions, elect to
change the amount, manner or form of distributions. The following
restrictions apply:
(a) The change must be an amount, manner, or
form permitted under the provisions of the Plan.
(b) Any change in amount must continue to
comply with the rules in Sections 7.02 and 7.03 and any other
applicable requirements of the code or Regulations.
(c) All changes must be made in writing and
are subject to the approval of the plan Administrator.
(d) No more than one election to change may
be made in any Plan Year.
(e) The Plan Administrator shall have
discretion to adopt appropriate rules for the administration
requests made under this Section 7.07.

Section 7.09 is amended by modifying the first sentence to read as follows:
“Notwithstanding any other provision of this Plan, the amount distributed to a
Participant for any calendar year in which the Participant’s Designated
Beneficiary is not the spouse must satisfy the Minimum Distribution Incidental
Benefit Requirement described in this Section.”

Old Section 7.09(a) is deleted.

Old Section 7.09(b)(1) is amended by modifying the first and second paragraphs
to read:
Distributions Not Paid from an Annuity Contract. For any calendar year
beginning after December 31, 1988, if benefits are not paid byway of an
insured annuity contract, the Minimum Distribution Requirement will be
satisfied if:
(1) distribution of a Participant’s benefit commences not later than April
1 of the calendar year following the later of the calendar year in which

<a id="ga28-p507"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=509 printed_page=507 -->

the Participant attains age 70 1/2, and the calendar year in which the
Participant retires from Service (the later of these years being identified
in this Section 7.09 as the “Base Year”); and.

Old Section 7.09(b) is further amended by replacing “calendar year in which the
Participant attains age 70 Vi" every place it appears after the first instance with
“Base Year”

Old Section 7.09(b)(2) is amended to read as follows
(b) Distributions Paid from an Annuity Contract. If benefits are paid
byway of an insured annuity contract, the Minimum Distribution
Requirement will be satisfied if:
(1) distribution of a Participant’s benefit commences not later
than April 1 of the calendar year following the base Year, and
(2) The amount distributed with respect to the Base Year and
each calendar year thereafter is not less than the minimum
amount required to be distributed under Section 401(a)(9) of the
Code and the Regulations thereunder

Section 8.01 is amended by replacing “Filing of a Claim” with “Filing of
Request”, and replacing “claim for Plan benefits” with “request for
distributions” .

Section 8.02 is amended by replacing “Denial of Claim” with “Denial of
Request”, replacing “claim” the next three places it appears with “request”, and
replacing “Plan’s claim procedure” with “Plan’s distribution request procedure”

Insert a new Section 12.16 to read as follows:
Separation from Service. for purposes of this Plan, a Participant who
is an ordained Teaching Elder in the Presbyterian Church in America
shall be considered to have separated or retired from Service, or from all
remunerative Service, with the Employer, or to have terminated Service
with the Employer, only when such Teaching Elder is either:
• no longer a member of an ypres byte ry of the Presbyterian Church in America, or
• Honorably retired from his Presbytery.
Any other Participant shall be considered to have so separated, retired, or
terminated whenever any employment of the participant wi than employer terminates.

The Entire Plan is amended by modifying its spacing, numbering, headings,
and style and correcting typographical and other errors, as reflected in the
revised Plan document presented to the Trustees

EXCERPTS FROM THE MINUTES
OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY OF THE
PRESBYTERIAN CHURCH IN AMERICA

WHEREAS, the board of trustees of the Presbyterian Church in America
Insurance, annuities and relief funds has approved adoption of the eighteenth and
Nineteenth Amendments to the Presbyterian Church in America Voluntary Tax-
Sheltered Annuity Plan and the Eleventh and Twelfth Amendments to the Presbyterian Church in America Money Purchase Pension Plan, subject to approval by the General Assembly,

WHEREAS, the General Assembly has determined that these amendments
recommended by the Trustees should be approved;

RESOLVED, that adoption of the amendments is hereby ratified by the General Assembly of the Presbyterian Church in America

THREE

FORMER STATEMENT OF PURPOSE

the purpose of the board of trustees of the Insurance, annuity and relief funds of the
Presbyterian Church in America is to glorify God through serving His servants who
minister in the Church by providing for the health and welfare, retirement and relief
needs of the denomination's ministers, missionaries, lay church workers and their
families.

REVISED STATEMENT OF PURPOSE

the purpose of the board of trustees of the Insurance, annuity and relief funds of the
Presbyterian Church in America is to glorify God through serving His servants who
minister in the Church by offering financial products to provide for the health and
welfare, retirement and relief needs of the denomination's ministers, missionaries, lay
church workers and their families, and to serve the churches, committees and agencies
of the PCA and related organizations by sponsoring and endorsing a denominational
property and liability insurance program.

## INTERCHURCH RELATIONS COMMITTEE REPORT TO THE TWENTY-EIGHTH GENERAL ASSEMBLY, 2000

### I. Membership

|  TE Derek W. H. Thomas | RE Robert Ashlock  |
| --- | --- |
|  TE R. Irfon Hughes, chrmn. | RE R. Meade Guy,  |
|  TE Craig R. Higgins | RE James D. Walters, Jr. sec.  |
|  TE M. Christopher Ehlers, alt. | RE David R. Lambert, alt.  |
|  TE L. Roy Taylor, Stated Clerk (RAO III, 3-2, j., ex officio) |   |
|  TE Ken Thompson (MTW Consultant, Advisory Member)  |   |

### II. Meetings

|  September 28, 1999 | Atlanta  |
| --- | --- |
|  November 8, 1999 | Conference call  |
|  February 21, 2000 | Conference call  |
|  April 10, 2000 | Conference Call  |

### III. Information Items

A. The PCA is a member of the North American Presbyterian and Reformed Council (NAPARC). IRC chairman, TE R. Irfon Hughes, and the PCA Stated Clerk attended the NAPARC meeting November 16-17, 1999 in Flat Rock, NC. The PCA Stated clerk was elected chairman. The council discussed the Proposal of Dr. Robert Godfrey to form a Non-judicial General Synod of Presbyterian and Reformed Churches. The council requested that all NAPARC denominations currently studying the role of women in the military report their findings to NAPARC. The council has funded the translation into Spanish of G. I. Williamson’s *The Westminster Confession of Faith for Study Classes* and a harmony of subjects in Reformed Confessional Standards.

B. The Christian Reformed Church of North America will reconsider its stand on the ordination of women at its General Synod 2000. NAPARC may reconsider the CRCNA’s relationship with NAPARC as a result.

C. The PCA is a member of the World Fellowship of Reformed Churches under the World Evangelical Fellowship through our membership in the NAE. No current IRC members are involved in the WFRC. PCA ministers and former IRC members, TE Eric Perrin and TE Paul Gilchrist, are officers in the WFRC (Dr. Perrin, President, Dr. Gilchrist Secretary). The WFRC meets once every four to six years. The WFRC is primarily Churches in North, Central, and South America. The WFRC has initiated contacts with the International Reformed Fellowship, which

<a id="ga28-p511"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=513 printed_page=511 -->

is primarily composed of Churches in Asia. Representatives of the two groups will meet in Orlando, FL this fall to discuss a possible merger of the two fellowships.

D. The committee approved instructions for fraternal delegates addressing the PCA General Assembly.

F. The committee requested Advisory member TE Ken Thompson to look into the possibility of the PCA considering membership in the International Council of Reformed Churches

## IV. Items Discussed and Actions Taken

A. Levels of fraternal relations with other denominations - The committee discussed the various possible categories of fraternal relations with other denominations and decided to simplify matters by having only two levels, i.e. Fraternal Relations and Corresponding Relations. (See recommendation below). It should be noted that other North American Presbyterian and Reformed Council (NAPARC) Churches have similar arrangements.

B. Overture 8-1999 from Philadelphia Presbytery the Proposal of Dr. Robert Godfrey to form a Non-judicial General Synod of Presbyterian and Reformed Churches. The committee recommended to NAPARC that the council seriously consider the proposal and invite Dr. Godfrey to speak to the issue. NAPARC met November 16-17 at Bonclarken hosted by the Associate Reformed Presbyterian Church (ARPC) and considered the issue. Representatives of the United Reformed Churches in North America (URCNA) were present as observers. Dr. Godfrey is a member of the URCNA. The URCNA observers reported that the URCNA General Synod had considered and not adopted the proposal. The consensus of the NAPRC members was that the proposed ecclesiastical body would not be a denomination but a council whose functions are now being fulfilled by NAPARC. NAPARC passed the following motion proposed by the ARPC:

We are grateful for the bonds of the Gospel and the Reformed Faith that unite us. In the light of “II. Basis of the Council” (Constitution of NAPARC) and in order that all NAPARC Churches may be more fully aware of the unique characteristics of each member Church, we recommend that each member Church discern and enumerate those issues of belief, practice and government that, to the best of their knowledge, distinguish them from other NAPARC Churches, and submit a report to NAPARC, no later than October 3, 2000 so that NAPARC may evaluate the biblical and confessional bases for such distinctive positions and the degree to which these issues necessitate continued separation.

<a id="ga28-p512"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=514 printed_page=512 -->

The PCA IRC is currently working on that request and will report to the
next General Assembly what concrete proposals, if any result from the
discussions from within NAPARC. It should be noted that a similar
proposal resulted in the joining and receiving of the reformed
Presbyterian Church Evangelical Synod into the PCA in 1982. The
present member churches of na parc are:
Associate Reformed Presbyterian Church - 34,642 members
Korean American Presbyterian Church - 33,863 members
Orthodox Presbyterian Church - 24,413 members
Presbyterian Church in America - 289,906 members
Reformed Church in the United States - 4,257 members
Reformed Presbyterian Church in North America - 6,195 members

Christian Reformed Church in North America - Suspended from voting

C. the PCA is a member of the national association of Evangelicals.
Neither IRC chairman, TE R Irfon Hughes, nor the PCA Stated Clerk
attended NAE annual convention due to budgetary and time restraints.
The NAE revised its bylaws this year to allow evangelical denominations
to hold dual membership in the NAE and the National Council of
Churches, provided the evangelical denominations affirm the NAE
doctrinal statement. the committee discussed the issue of the national
association of Evangelical’s revising Bylaw B-7 as follows:

B-7 Dual Membership Although denominations shall not hold dual
membership- in the national association of evangelicals and any
other organization at a comparable ecclesiastical level, -such as the
national council of churches of christ in America, the World
council of Churches, and the American -Council -o f - Christian
Churches, local churches and organizations whose denominations
who belong to other such bodies but who desire membership in the
national association of evangelicals maybe received provided they
fulfill membership requirements. Denominations mav hold dual
membership in the national association of evangelicals and other
organizations at a comparable ecclesiastical level. Denominations
holding membership in other such organizations mav be received
into the NAE provided they fulfill the membership requirements.

the stated clerk reported that he had contacted the president of then ae
about the rule change and had received the following response from the
president’s office:

In a major development signaling change, the NAE voted to change
its by-laws so that member denominations of other bodies, willing to
sign its seven-point statement of faith, would be eligible to join the

<a id="ga28-p513"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=515 printed_page=513 -->

evangelical association. Allowing such membership, the NAE said is now possible because our conservative theological identity is stronger than ever. “Whereas we have often been defined relative to the more theologically liberal National Council of Churches, we recognize that our identity is no longer based on being compared to them,” a convention release stated.

It was decided that

- The IRC inform the General Assembly of the recent change in NAE Bylaw B-7, “Dual Membership.”
- The IRC inform the General Assembly the IRC has directed the Stated Clerk to communicate our concerns regarding the rule change to the President of the NAE.
- The IRC request the General Assembly to allow the IRC to study for one year, the issue of the NAE Bylaw B-7, “Dual Membership,” and report to the 2001 General Assembly.

Through its association with the NAE, the PCA has contacts as a Reformed denomination with other evangelical denominations, is a member of the World Evangelical Fellowship and the World Fellowship of Reformed Churches, and participates in World Relief, an international mercy ministry. Evangelical individuals, local churches, regional ecclesiastical bodies, denominations, and para-church ministries may join the NAE.

D. Overture 8 from Illiana Presbytery, “Evangelical Presbyterian Church Receiving Suspended PCA Teaching Elder” was referred to the IRC. (See recommendation below). The committee considered Overture 8, concerning the Evangelical Presbyterian Church Receiving a Suspended PCA Teaching Elder. It was noted that the Stated Clerk of the General Assembly, at the request of Illiana presbytery, had written the Stated Clerk of the EPC Presbytery of Mid-America and the Stated Clerk of the General Assembly of the EPC regarding the matter, sending copies of the SJC Decision on Case 98-9, the Appeal of TE David Baer vs. Illiana Presbytery. The Stated Clerk of the EPC Presbytery of Mid-America had written to the Stated Clerk of Illiana Presbytery the following on April 6:

I have received the following opinion from the Stated Clerk of the EPC General Assembly concerning Rev. David Baer and Illiana Presbytery. “It is my opinion, after considered study, that Mid-America Presbytery (EPC) acted within its constitutional rights as defined in the Book of Government 13-5 to receive the Rev. Mr. Baer into its membership. This provisional opinion shall be forwarded to the appropriate parties with both the Presbytery and

<a id="ga28-p514"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=516 printed_page=514 -->

General Assembly of our sister denomination, the Presbyterian Church in America.”

it was noted that the stated clerk of the pc aha snot yet received
any direct response to his communications tot heep ca soft he date of
the writing of this report. The committee decided to recommend to the
General Assembly a response to Overture 8. (See recommendation
below).

RECOMMENDATIONS
1 That Fraternal Delegates, Corresponding Delegates, and Ecclesiastical
Observers be welcomed and invited to address the General Assembly.
2. That visiting ministers be introduced to the General Assembly (BCO 13-
13).
3. that the General Assembly establish two levels of relations with other
denominations:
a. Fraternal Relations - The General Assembly may maintain a
fraternal relationship with other Presbyterian/Reformed
denominations that are voting members of the north american
Presbyterian and Reformed Council and with other such
Churches with whom the General Assembly wishes to establish
fraternal relations unilaterally. This would involve the exchange
of fraternal delegates, exchange of General Assembly or general
Synod minutes, communications on matters of mutual concern,
and other matters that may arise from time to time.
b. Corresponding relations - The General Assembly may maintain
corresponding relation with other evangelical Churches in North
America and in other continents for exchanging greetings and
letters of encouragement. This may include the exchange of
official observers at the broadest assemblies, and
communications on issues of common concern.
4.IRC requests the General Assembly to allow the IRC to study for one
year, the issue of then ae bylaw B-7, “Dual Membership,” and report
to the 2001 General Assembly.
5. That Overture 8-2000 from Illiana Presbytery, “Evangelical
Presbyterian Church Receiving a Suspended PCA Teaching Elder” be
answered by the General Assembly’s adopting the following response to

##### Overture 8:

• that the General Assembly of the Presbyterian Church in America communicate to the General Assembly of the
Evangelical Presbyterian Church that the PCA is disappointed
that the presbytery of Mid-America has apparently ignored the
judicial process of the PCA.

<a id="ga28-p517"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=28 pdf_page=517 printed_page=null -->

that the General Assembly of the Presbyterian Church in America requests that the Evangelical Presbyterian Church honor
the discipline of the PCA.
